source: Invest Like the Best — AI, semiconductors, and the robotic frontier (EP.385)
summary: BCI (Brain-Computer Interface) will be the next true compute platform after smartphones, with augmented reality glasses as the intermediate step
summary_extended: Baker argues that the next major compute platform transition will follow a path from smartphones to augmented reality glasses to brain-computer interfaces (BCI), with VR goggles being a dead end that will not achieve mass adoption. He contends that AR glasses paired with ambient computing represent the intermediate step, while BCI — direct neural-digital interaction — is the ultimate long-term end-state for human-computer interaction, echoing science fiction concepts of human-AI hybrids. Meta is specifically cited as a key player given its acquisition of CTRL Labs, a BCI startup, positioning it as the most identifiable public equity with direct exposure to this thesis. Baker presents no specific timeline or financial metrics, framing this as a highly speculative, decade-plus structural view rather than a near-term investment call. The investment implication is loosely that Meta deserves attention as the only large-cap public company with meaningful BCI R&D assets, though Baker stops short of making it an explicit buy recommendation on this basis alone.
detail: Baker argues VR goggles will not succeed but augmented reality glasses and ambient computing will, with BCI as the ultimate end-state for human-computer interaction. Meta controls CTRL Labs and is working on BCI. The phone will remain the compute layer for extended reality. This is the 'high-man' (human-AI hybrid) future from science fiction.
confidence_evidence: Baker presents this as a long-term structural view without specific investment implications or timeline
quote_fragment: BCI may be the next true way we interact with computers after phones

ticker_audit.reasoning: Meta is directly named as the company controlling CTRL Labs and actively working on BCI technology, making it the primary public equity subject of this thesis.
